Shah MD,Meika R (3/18/2026 12:59 PDT); Villarama APRN,
Joseph R (3/18/2026 06:38 PDT); Villarama APRN,Joseph R
Pt had a liver biopsy 1 week ago, nausea/voming today. PMH Hep C, afib, pacemaker,
Patient is a 74-year-old female with history of hep C, A-fib, pacemaker, presents to the
ED for complaint of abdominal pain. She also states having a recent liver biopsy 2
weeks ago outpatient for her hep C. Patient reports having right upper quadrant
abdominal pain that radiates to her back that started in the morning around 11 AM.
She states that she had a similar episode 1 week ago that resolved on its own however
today since 11 AM the pain has worsened and decided to come into the ED. She states
that it occurred after eating some spicy noodles. When she got brought in by EMS,
EMS gave her fentanyl and Zofran but did not find any relief. She otherwise denies any
other complaints at this time. She states after getting medications in the ED, her pain
has improved. Denies any alcohol use or drug use, reports being a former smoker.
Vitals in the ED show patient to be hypertensive that since resolved. Labs show WBC
16, platelets 456, glucose 145, gap 15, T. bili 3.9, alk phos 590, AST 203, ALT 83,
lipase 2418. CTAP showed common bile duct dilatation with associated dilatation of the
H/O transfusion of packed red blood cells
main pancreatic duct, concerning for possible pancreatic head mass, also showed
peripancreatic edema consistent with pancreatitis. She was given Zofran, Dilaudid,
Toradol in the ED. ED added patient to the GI list. Patient being admitted for
PCP: Smith PA at Green Valley Medical Center
A 14 point review of systems was performed and was negative with the exceptions
Numeric Pain Scale: 10 = Worst possible pain (22:10)
General: Alert, VSS, and in no acute cardiopulmonary distress.
